strengths: 20 years of diverse software engineering experience exceeding the 15-year minimum, with proven expertise across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Current role involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, demonstrating recent engagement with AI ecosystem and agentic workflows
Demonstrated ability to inspire and teach through multiple workshops, courses, and community leadership (Refactoring Dojos, Selenium courses, Web Internals workshop)
concerns: No recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated AI-powered products or platforms built with AI; current work is infrastructure/tooling-focused rather than strategic product-focused
No evidence of strategic technology advisory, cloud product strategy experience, or identifying differentiation opportunities for customers—core responsibilities of the role
No advanced degree in AI-related field and no track record of Director-level strategic leadership; background is strong IC craftsman (Staff Software Engineer) not Principal strategist
response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior software engineer with 20 years of experience and recent exposure to AI infrastructure through MCP frameworks and agentic workflows. However, the Principal Engineer—AI Strategy role explicitly requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI' and strategic responsibilities like advising customers on technology adoption and identifying product differentiation. The résumé shows no AI product development, ML systems, or AI-powered platform experience—only infrastructure tooling. The career trajectory emphasizes engineering craftsmanship, testing practices, and infrastructure work (Staff-level IC focus), not strategic technology advisory or innovation leadership required at Principal/Director level.
